TextENGNew: How long had I been lying there?
Outside, it was already getting dark.
My body was drenched in sweat.@k ...I should probably change into some sleepwear.
changeReason: Old says 'change my sheets'; new says 'change into some sleepwear.' JPN 寝巻きに着替えた means 'change into sleepwear/pajamas' -- old translated the wrong object entirely

TextENGNew: Just then, the phone rang downstairs.
It was probably Mom.
She was quite the worrywart.@k ...It might not even have been the first call—I may have just slept through the earlier ones.
``Hello, Maebara residence.``
``Oh, you're alive! Hello!@k@vS01/03/120300083. It's Mion.@k@vS01/03/120300084. How are you feeling~?``
changeReason: Old says 'Oh, he's alive!'; new says 'Oh, you're alive!' JPN お、生きてた is ambiguous but in phone context, addressing the person directly ('you') vs referring to someone in third person ('he') changes the interaction dynamic

TextENGNew: ``Oh, is this one of Keiichi's friends?@k@vS01/01/120100321. Keiichi's resting right now, but...@k@vS01/01/120100322. Shall I put him on?``
``Huh?!@k@vS01/03/120300086. O-Oh, is this his father??@k@vS01/03/120300087. Ahahahaha, s-s-sorry!!``
``Bwahahahahaha!!@k@vS01/01/120100324. Sucker~!@k@vS01/01/120100325. You totally fell for it!``
``What?!@k@vS01/03/120300089. K-Kei-chaaaaAAAAAaan!!``
We fell into our usual routine of banter and shared a laugh.
...But even as we laughed together, I couldn't stop the suspicion creeping in...
...Was everyone... really connected to those incidents...?
...It saddened me that I couldn't bring myself to say 'no.'

``I'm relieved you sound well.@k@vS01/03/120300091. Rena was really worried, you know?@k@vS01/03/120300092. Satoko was gloating—'serves him right!'@k@vS01/03/120300093. And Rika-chan said, 'poor thing, being sick!'``
``Tch, sounds like you all had a grand old time without me!``
changeReason: Old says 'everyone was making a big deal out of it'; new says 'you all had a grand old time without me.' JPN implies envy/being left out, which new captures; old misses the 'without me' nuance

TextENGNew: ``Now, now. So, Kei-chan.@k@vS01/03/120300095. Would it be a bad time if we stopped by?``
``A visit?@k@vS01/01/120100328. Nah, you don't have to. I'm not exactly on my deathbed.``
``My grandma made a mountain of ohagi, you see.@k@vS01/03/120300097. She told me to share them around.@k@vS01/03/120300098. So I'm bringing some over!``
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi (sweet rice balls coated in red bean paste), a different item from mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: ``...Hey, don't blame me if you catch my cold.``
``Don't worry! Both Rena and this old man are too dumb to catch colds!@k@vS01/03/120300100. See ya!@k@vS01/03/120300101. We'll be right there!``
She hung up with characteristic energy.
Rena was coming too?
That's what it sounded like.
...Seeing Rena still made me a bit uneasy, but I figured it would be fine if Mion was with her.
Less than ten minutes later, the doorbell rang.
``How are you feeling?@k@vS01/03/120300103. Did you take your medicine and rest properly~?``
``You bet I did.``
``I-I'm glad you look okay. I was worried...``
``Sorry about that. ...I'll be fine by tomorrow.``
Mion, who showed absolutely no concern for someone recovering from illness, and Rena, who looked genuinely worried from the bottom of her heart.
...Their expressions seemed sincere.
``...I was going to hang out for a bit, but it looks like you're still not back to full strength.``
``Hm, r-really?!``
It seemed they'd noticed the shadow crossing my face.
``...Well, here, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS01/02/120200146. ...These are ohagi that Mii-chan's grandma made.``
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: Rena held out a bundle wrapped in newspaper.
There were probably about five inside.
...It had a satisfying heft to it.
``Oh, thanks. ...Give my regards to your grandma, Mion.``
``Sure.@k@vS01/02/120200148. Oh, and there's one I made mixed in there too!@k@vS01/02/120200149. I wonder if Keiichi-kun can figure out which one...``
``This is Kei-chan's homework for missing today's club meeting!@k@vS01/03/120300106. Each ohagi has a letter on it, so give me your answer tomorrow!``
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: ``I-Is this a get-well visit or a club assignment—make up your mind!!!``
``Yep, yep, looking lively. You'll be fine by tomorrow~``
``What are you gonna do if my fever comes back because of all this racket?``
``M-Mii-chan, we shouldn't make too much noise...@k@vS01/02/120200151. Let's go.@k@vS01/02/120200152. His family will get upset.``
...The two of them seemed to think my parents were home.
Our entryway was cluttered enough to give that impression.
``You're right, let's head out.@k@vS01/03/120300109. ...Oh, that's right, Kei-chan.``
``What?``
``What did you have for lunch?``
I flinched and looked up at Mion—and froze.
...It was an expression I'd never seen on her before. Deeply unsettling.
But... why... was she asking me about lunch...?
Mion's words were eerily hollow.
changeReason: Old says 'so inane it was almost meaningless' (trivial); new says 'eerily hollow' (ominous). JPN 無気味なくらい空虚 means 'hollow to an eerie/unsettling degree,' new captures the ominous tone the old misses

TextENGNew: ...The way she said it, she didn't seem to care about my answer in the slightest.
``I-I ate out...``
...Had they figured out I'd been with Ooishi-san...?
I knew that hesitating or dodging the question would only make them read more into it... so I answered as quickly as I could.
But... despite my best efforts, there was a pause before they responded.
``...Hmm, so you went out for lunch, Keiichi-kun.``
At some point, the light in Rena's eyes had changed too.
...They gleamed with an intensity that made the casual tone of her words feel all the more hollow.
...She already knew. That's the only way it looked to me—she was feigning ignorance.
``How was it? Tasty?``
``...Wh-Why are you even asking me this...?``
Mion's voice had dropped to an oddly low register.
...Almost as if... she knew I'd eaten at that restaurant in town...
No... I was overthinking this.
...Both of them should have been at school during lunch.
...There was no way they could have known where I was...
``Seems you were with a rather distinguished older gentleman. ...Who was he?``
*Thud.*
...The bundle of ohagi slipped from my hands.
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: I could feel the blood draining from my face with an almost audible rush.
``...Oh?@k@vS01/02/120200155. Keiichi-kun, who was that?@k@vS01/02/120200156. ...Could it have been the same man from before, I wonder?@k@vS01/02/120200157. ...I wonder?!``
My mouth went bone-dry, right down to the root of my tongue...
This was no bluff.
...They... knew everything...?!
``H-How... do you... know that...?``
It took everything I had to squeeze those words from my throat.
...My knees had started to buckle.
``...Who knows?@k@vS01/03/120300114. ...There's nothing this old man doesn't find out.``
...Mion smirked—a knowing, lingering grin.
``So, Kei-chan, what did you talk about?@k@vS01/03/120300116. You seemed pretty worked up about it...``
``W-We weren't talking about you guys...!@k@vS01/01/120100341. It had nothing to do with Mion or Rena...!``
``Hmm?@k@vS01/02/120200159. ...Funny how our names come up even though nobody asked about us, hmmm?``
Rena's sticky gaze bored into me, probing past my eyes into something deeper.
I'd dug my own grave.@k ...My heart pounded so hard it felt like it might explode...
``Well, whatever you're trying to hide, this old man already sees through it all.@k@vS01/03/120300118. ...Just don't forget that.``
I couldn't even shake my head.
...It took everything I had just to keep my teeth from chattering...
Mion tilted her head in an almost elegant gesture, but her gaze never once released mine.
``...Keiichi-kun, you don't look so good.@k@vS01/02/120200161. I think you should lie down.``
``You're right. We should be going.``
The two of them shared a laugh as if nothing had happened and turned to leave.
I hadn't moved a muscle since I'd dropped the ohagi—I stood frozen in the same position.
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: They stepped out through the door... and it began to close... slowly.
...All I could do was stare.@k ...As if I weren't allowed to move until the door had shut completely.
Then the nearly-closed door was yanked back open, and my heart leapt into my throat.
A single eye peered through the gap, and once again, Mion's hawkish gaze pierced right through me.
``See you, Kei-chan.``
``...'Don't you dare' miss school tomorrow, okay?``
*Slam.*
...The door closed.
Even after their low laughter faded into the distance and silence returned, I couldn't move a single finger...
When I finally came to my senses, the first thing I did was lock the door...
Mion and the others... knew I'd been talking to Ooishi-san...?!
changeReason: Old says 'They knew what Ooishi-san and I talked about'; new says 'Mion and the others knew I'd been talking to Ooishi-san.' Old implies they know the content, new only implies they know the meeting occurred. JPN uses 話していたこと (the fact of talking), ambiguous

TextENGNew: How...?!@k Why?!
No... that didn't matter. The only thing that mattered was that they knew.
Thinking back... from the very first time Ooishi-san approached me, they may have been listening to everything.@k ...Just as Mion said... I couldn't hide anything from them...
Then... what were they trying to tell me...?
...The answer was obvious.
They were warning me not to discuss anything 'unnecessary.'
What counts as unnecessary...?
I only ever discussed one thing with Ooishi-san.@k ...And that was what they were warning me about.

What Ooishi-san and I discussed... was that the annual incidents attributed to Oyashiro-sama's curse weren't isolated cases—@k they were all connected, and the perpetrators might be a group hiding within Hinamizawa itself...
No... he went further than that.
...He named Mion, Rena, Satoko, and Rika-chan outright as suspects.
If this was a warning against that...?
``Wh-What kind of nonsense am I thinking?!``
I slapped my own cheek so hard that it cracked through the air.
...I'd hoped it might snap me out of this nightmare... but for some reason it felt like hitting a pillow—frustratingly painless.
Calm down, Maebara Keiichi...!
Since when did I become someone who could only think the worst?!
Calm down. Calm down!
Take a breath and think this through...!
The reason Mion knew about my lunch with Ooishi-san... was probably just that someone from Hinamizawa happened to be at the restaurant.
They must have mentioned to Mion that they'd seen me there...
That was the most natural explanation.
And... when I really thought about it... all Mion had asked was where I ate lunch.
...Then she just asked, 'Was it good?'...!
Since the person I was with wasn't from Hinamizawa, it naturally piqued their curiosity... that's all. There wasn't necessarily any ulterior motive...@k That's right. That had to be it...!
When I thought about it, the same was true with Rena.
I'd been evasive about meeting Ooishi-san, so Rena had simply called me out on it.
The gap between her usual mild-mannered self and that sharp side had just thrown me off... it had simply startled me.@k That was the most natural way to look at it...!
...My head felt like a tangled mess of spaghetti.
...If you squeezed it, bright red marinara might come pouring out of my ears and nose.
That thought triggered a sudden wave of nausea and a headache.
changeReason: Old says 'felt like vomiting'; new says 'triggered a sudden wave of nausea and a headache.' JPN 嘔吐感 and context -- new adds 'headache' (頭痛) which may come from adjacent text, but it adds a symptom not in old

TextENGNew: ...Not wanting that to actually happen, I stopped clutching my head...
Lately, I had no idea what anyone was really saying...
Being with everyone was fun.@k There was no sense of hidden agendas.@k They really were good people...
When I'd just moved here, knowing nothing about anything, they'd been genuinely kind to me.
Rena was truly kind and thoughtful.@k When that strange side of her didn't show up, she was really a sweet girl.
Mion was also a fantastic person.@k She never cared about age or gender—positive and proactive.@k There was never a dull moment with her.
...Speaking of never dull, the boisterous Satoko was great too.@k She was mouthy, sure, but that was just how she connected with people.
...And Rika-chan was no different.@k She may not have talked much, but she wasn't withdrawn. ...She was one of us!
...But... ever since Tomitake-san and Ooishi-san had burdened me with Hinamizawa's secrets I didn't need to know—the story of Oyashiro-sama's curse—everything had started to go wrong.
And then Ooishi-san had told me... that everyone—Mion, Rena, Satoko, Rika-chan—was suspicious.
...After that... everyone changed.
...That's right.
Everything started going wrong after Ooishi-san began filling my head with those stories...
...I should have refused to listen back then. I should have shut it all out.
...For that matter, I should never have asked Tomitake-san about the past incidents on the night of the Watanagashi...
...If only I hadn't let that curiosity get the better of me... if only I hadn't...
That's right...@k That's why they did what they did to Tomitake-san.@k He was an outsider who had the nerve to tell me what they'd gone out of their way to keep hidden.
Then Ooishi-san would be next, too...@k For trying to dig up what everyone had warned me was better left buried.
changeReason: Old says 'They'll probably **** Ooishi-san as well' (censored violent action); new says 'Then Ooishi-san would be next, too...' (vaguer threat). The register and specificity differ significantly

TextENGNew: Besides... he was unforgivable for planting seeds of doubt about my friends.@k ...A man like that—who cares what happens to him...!!!

Tomitake-san and Ooishi-san were outsiders in the end.@k Entities that could never coexist with the people of Hinamizawa.@k People like them deserved to fall victim to Oyashiro-sama's curse and just ***!!
Everyone was innocent.
...The one at fault was me, for not being able to rein in my curiosity.
Everyone was innocent.
Everyone was innocent.
Everyonewasinnocenteveryonewasinnocenteveryonewasinnocent.

A dull, dazed feeling settled over me... like the grogginess of just waking up, but the ominous chill that had gripped me was fading.@k ...I was fine now.@k ...I wasn't scared anymore.@k ...I was completely back to normal.
I'd go to school tomorrow, bright and cheerful.@k I'd greet everyone.@k I'd join in club activities.@k It would be fun.@k It had to be fun.
...Because I was one of them.
``...Oh, right. I need to eat those ohagi...@k@vS01/01/120100346. I have to figure out which one Rena made.``
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: I remembered the 'homework' the two of them had brought along with their visit.
Though really, the fact that our club offered delivery service was pretty impressive.
I picked up the bundle of ohagi from where it had fallen on the floor and carried it to the living room.
It would be nice to have some tea with it.
...Homemade ohagi with a cup of tea.@k ...Hey.@k That was actually a pretty delightful setup.
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: Inside the newspaper wrapping were five compact but dense koshi-an ohagi.
changeReason: Old says 'red bean paste mochi'; new says 'koshi-an ohagi.' JPN こしあんのおはぎ means ohagi with smooth bean paste (koshi-an), an entirely different food preparation from mochi

TextENGNew: Letters had been written on the newspaper beneath each one, from left to right: A, B, C, D, E.
Now then.@k One of these was supposedly Rena's handiwork... but which one?
They all looked more or less the same.
The smell and general appearance were nearly identical.@k ...This was... a genuinely challenging problem!
If there was one area where differences might show... it was probably the shape.
I had no idea what kind of person Mion's grandmother was, but her work had to be different from Rena's...!
Looking closely, I noticed one ohagi that had been crafted with exceptional care.
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: If you stared at them, you could spot it right away.
``...Looks like the whole thing hinges on whether or not this one is Rena's.``
I calmed myself and thought harder...!
...Mion had said her grandma made a mountain of them and was sharing them around.
...Which meant four of these five came from that massive batch.
changeReason: Old says 'four of these' without context; new says 'four of these five,' explicitly stating the total count of items being compared. JPN この中の４つ in context of five ohagi makes '4 of 5' more accurate

TextENGNew: And what about Rena?
She'd probably... only made this single one.
So she would have poured all her effort into that one piece.
...In other words... Rena's handmade ohagi was... this one—E!!
changeReason: Old says 'the one Rena had made was this one... E!!'; new says 'Rena's handmade ohagi was... this one--E!!' Also corrects mochi to ohagi

TextENGNew: For a moment, I wondered if Mion had anticipated this and set a trap, but that was unlikely.
...If Mion had made it, I'd be less certain, but since she'd said Rena made it, there shouldn't be any trick.
``All right.@k@vS01/01/120100349. Tea is ready.@k@vS01/01/120100350. First up, the defending champion—Mion's grandma.@k@vS01/01/120100351. ...Let's see.``
Hmm.@k ...Not bad.
The smoothness of the koshi-an paired with its substantial bite was not to be underestimated!!
A sip of tea afterward only enhanced the experience.@k ...This was... exquisite craftsmanship!
Now, how about Rena's?
A delicate creation worthy of a high-end Japanese confectionery.
As a growing boy, I was a little concerned about the smaller portion...@k But first, a bite...
...This was going to be a tough call!
Since the ingredients were identical, the flavor was virtually the same.
The only difference was in the final shaping, so that was to be expected.
So the deciding factors would be artistry... and portion size.
The champion, with her satisfying, substantial portion, versus the challenger Rena, with her irresistibly dainty size!
I'd only taken one bite of Rena's so far.
Better to finish it before passing judgment.
...There might be some incredible surprise hidden inside that could turn the tide...!
``*Munch munch* ...Hmm?``
It seemed my prediction had been right.
My tongue touched something.
It didn't feel like food, so I pinched it between my fingers and pulled it out.
...What...@k@|@y ...was this?
Before my brain could process what it was, I'd already hurled the half-eaten ohagi with all my strength!!!
changeReason: Old says 'mochi'; new says 'ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Corrected food item

TextENGNew: It smashed against the wall, and bean paste splattered everywhere.
After a single heartbeat, it peeled away and dropped to the floor.
...I stared, stunned at my own actions.
What was I doing...?!
Rena had gone to the trouble of making it by hand... how could I?!
Dazed, I looked at the hand that had committed this atrocity... then remembered what I had pulled from my mouth...
At first, I'd thought it was a strand of hair.
But even Rena, whose hair was shorter than Mion's, had fairly long hair.@k It wouldn't have been this short.
Besides, it was a bit too stiff to be hair.
It was thick enough to roll around on my tongue.
...It had a faint silver gleam, and at one end... yes, there was a small hole—like the eye of a sewing needle...
Yes, that was it...
It looked very much like a sewing needle.
...Exactly like one.
...The tip was sharp, too.@k Extremely sharp.
...It truly did look just like a sewing needle.
Wait.
...That thing just now... if it only 'looked like' a sewing needle... then what was it...?
...I couldn't answer.
But another part of me already knew—and it told me by setting my molars chattering.
...I couldn't push back the terror welling up inside me...
A metallic taste flooded my mouth, and the back of my tongue began to sting.
...I shoved a finger into the back of my mouth, feeling around to check for bleeding.
...A sudden surge of nausea.
The burning tang of stomach acid seared the back of my throat.
I clamped both hands over my mouth and writhed in agony... until the nausea finally, barely, subsided.
Once I could breathe normally again, my heart started hammering like a fire bell.
...And then, finally, the conscious part of me understood too.@k What had been hidden inside the ohagi.
changeReason: Old says 'mixed in with the mochi'; new says 'hidden inside the ohagi.' JPN おはぎ means ohagi, not mochi. Also 'mixed in' vs 'hidden inside' changes the nature of the concealment

TextENGNew: My hands moved before the word even formed in my mind.
*SPLAT!*
*SMACK!!*
*SPLORCH!!!*
I hurled the remaining ohagi against the wall, one after another.
changeReason: Old says 'mochi' (wrong food item); new says 'ohagi' (correct). JPN おはぎ means ohagi (rice balls), not mochi

TextENGNew: The geometric patterns the scattered bean paste drew on the wall seemed to suggest something ominous...
I tore my eyes away, dashed into the hallway, thundered up the stairs to my room, and dove under the covers that were still laid out from this morning.
Clutching my own shoulders, I howled—a snarl of fear, frustration, rage, and grief all tangled together...
This wasn't a warning, or a threat, or an admonition—nothing so mild as any of those...!!!
What had happened in Hinamizawa?
What was happening now?
What was about to happen?
...I didn't know the answer to any of those. And none of them mattered.
What taboo had I broken, and where?!
All I knew was... Rena and Mion... and perhaps others too?@k I had made enemies of them!!
And they... wanted me dead!!!
Like hell I'll let you kill me!!@k Not like this—not without even knowing why!!!
A vortex of negative emotions dragged me down into a restless sleep, like a swirling, bottomless swamp...
